events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate sequence violation: service disconnect pulled but no wait time/verification. Work in progress. High-voltage safety. Decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk critical. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions installed-defective? The observed field condition is service disconnect pulled but no wait time/verification (SEQ). Condition categories may apply installed-defective. Could include non-compliant? The schema component_conditions list includes installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. Since sequence violation, maybe non-compliant. But scenario says component condition categories that may apply: installed-defective. The service disconnect condition? It is pulled but no wait/verification. I'd include installed-defective? Maybe not exactly component defective, but the state is non-compliant. Need decide. The task: check order of work and catch sequence violation. Findings: service disconnect pulled but mandatory wait/verification omitted; residual HV energy may remain; work proceeding before safe state verified. Actions: stop work immediately; wait required OEM-specified time after disconnect; verify zero energy with qualified HV meter / absence of voltage test on all sources/capacitors; don appropriate PPE; retrain technician; document verification before continuing. Rationale: pulling disconnect alone doesn't ensure de-energization; HV systems store lethal energy; OEM/NFPA require lockout/tagout, wait, verify. References: OEM HV procedures, NFPA 70E-adjacent. percent_complete maybe 10. value not asked 0. sound_source etc default empty/zero. order maybe empty. workable empty. Need produce valid JSON. Ensure no prose outside.
